summary: P13 acceptance is retracted. P13 proved the system-internal business app/module/runtime record flow, but did not prove the prototype flow for platform-level external apps used for exposing system data or capabilities through OpenAPI.

issues.problem: The term app is ambiguous across PRD, API and UI. The current UI treats app mainly as a system-internal business modeling container, while the prototype feedback points to platform-level external apps for OpenAPI/data exposure.
issues.impact: Users cannot understand how to use the system to expose configured system data or capabilities to external consumers.
issues.requiredAction: Freeze a product glossary and prototype flow traceability matrix before further frontend acceptance.

issues.problem: The current browser E2E covers system-internal modeling and runtime records, but not create external app, authorize modules/fields/actions, generate secret, external call, and audit log review.
issues.impact: A passing P13 E2E is insufficient evidence for the actual prototype flow.
issues.requiredAction: Rework UI/UX and frontend after terminology is frozen, then add browser E2E plus OpenAPI call evidence.

issues.problem: PM over-accepted local build/package and a partial business flow as full user usability.
issues.impact: The project process did not catch a core product concept mismatch before user deployment feedback.
issues.requiredAction: AGENTS.md now requires glossary freeze and prototype-flow traceability gates for future phases.

verification: docs/user_requirement.md contains both system-internal app configuration and OpenAPI external app requirements.
.codex/oldexamine/examine-web/src/main/java/com/unique/examine/web/controller/PlatformAppController.java shows an old platform-level external app entry.
docs/ui/ui-design.md currently designs app configuration as system-internal app -> module -> field -> page -> publish.
docs/test_runs/p13-usability-e2e.md proves a system-internal modeling/runtime flow, not the platform external app flow.
